summary: High‑quality matrix mix captures a solid spring‑89 show highlighted by a smoking Playin’ → Built to Last transition, fiery Scarlet Fire jam and an inspiring Other One.
review: The recording is a well‑balanced SBD‑matrix source that sounds clear and full, especially in the low end where Phil’s bass shines, though some listeners note Jerry’s vocals and guitar get buried at times. Musically the concert is strong – the second set delivers a seamless Playin’/Built to Last segue, an expansive Birdsong, a blazing Scarlet > Fire jam and a powerful Other One that many reviewers call the night’s high point.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently positive 4‑5 star reviews praise both performance (standout jams and transitions) and recording clarity; minor mix quirks keep the rating just shy of perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced matrix mix with excellent bass presence (great for sub‑woofer listening). Some reviewers mention Jerry’s voice/guitar being too low in the mix, but overall sound is crisp and listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds nicely with a strong Birdsong; second set shines thanks to the seamless Playin’ → Built to Last transition, extended Scarlet > Fire jam and a soaring Other One that keep the momentum high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals noted as slightly strained and sometimes buried in the mix; guitar work praised when audible.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is prominent and rich, especially enjoyable on sub‑woofers; many reviewers highlight his strong performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Rhythm guitar and vocals heard clearly; contributes well to the overall interplay.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards stand out in the jams, adding texture and depth.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming solid and drives transitions (e.g., drums → Playin’), no major complaints.
